role_title: Staff Growth Product Manager, Lyft Business

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: Multi-year growth strategy ownership, shared infrastructure across products
Represents Lyft Business Growth in exec reviews and cross-org forums

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience, with a meaningful portion owning growth, lifecycle, or funnel work at scale.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful portion in growth, lifecycle, or funnel work at scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Track record of measurable, durable impact on acquisition, activation, or retention metrics that you can defend.
Demonstrated ownership of broad, ambiguous problem areas that span teams. You have set a multi-year growth strategy and built shared infrastructure that compounded across products.
Quantitative rigor and experimentation discipline. You think in funnels, cohorts, and growth loops. You size opportunities, design clean A/B tests, and read results without overclaiming.
Fluency with the modern growth stack: lifecycle and CRM platforms, attribution, product analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar), and experimentation tooling.
Comfortable in B2B, B2B2C, or marketplace contexts with multiple customer types whose interests do not always align.
Exceptional communication and influence at every altitude, from a growth engineer debating instrumentation to a VP sponsoring the revenue target. You move organizations to act.

scope.surface: Lyft Business growth — acquisition, activation, lifecycle, growth loops, marketing and lifecycle stack across B2B and B2B2C surfaces (admin portal, rider app, partner surfaces)
scope.business_model: B2B and B2B2C — transportation solutions for organizations including business travel, employee commutes, courtesy rides, and non-emergency medical trips
primary_metrics_inferred: Activation rate / time to first value
Experiment velocity and validated test rate
core_responsibilities: Own demand generation and build the acquisition playbook across channels, tactics, and mar-tech
Own activation in partnership with vertical PMs to drive customers to first value
Architect the journey from awareness through landing in admin portal, rider app, and partner surfaces
Design and operate growth loops converting acquired customers into channels for new acquisition
Set the experimentation cadence, shipping bar, and readout standards
Translate funnel data, cohort behavior, and experiment results into prioritization
Represent Lyft Business Growth in exec reviews and cross-org forums

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K–$220K base in NYC area) and competitive for Staff PM level
Clear product surface and scope — Lyft Business Growth with well-defined responsibilities
Lyft Business described as 'one of Lyft's fastest-growing and most profitable units'
Strong cross-functional team including Engineers, Designers, Data Scientists, and Lifecycle/Product Marketers
Hybrid flexibility with up to 4 weeks per year working from anywhere
Generous benefits including 18 weeks paid parental leave and 401(k) match
red_flags: Hybrid role requires in-office 3 days per week (Mon, Wed, Thu) — limited flexibility for those not in NYC
Role is NYC-area specific; no mention of remote eligibility or other city options
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for non-US-authorized candidates
8+ years PM experience with meaningful growth focus is a high bar that eliminates most generalist PMs

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title, 8+ years requirement, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — no direct reports mentioned in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)

Equity is mentioned as 'potential equity offering' but the form (RSUs, options, etc.) is not specified.
